media.text:
Your order is out for delivery 🚀
 
At 6:05pm ET (2205 UTC) on May 15, our next @SpaceX resupply mission lifted off to the @Space_Station and will reach its destination on May 17. go.nasa.gov/4eKxaRk
media.author: NASA

media.text:
Today, a resupply mission launched to the space station aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket bearing the @Freedom250 logo.

This mission carries several special items to celebrate this historic milestone, including semiquincentennial commemorative coins from the @USTreasury and @usmint, along with multiple sets of new @USPS stamps, and our own NASA Freedom 250 patches.

For more information about our celebration of America’s 250th year, visit nasa.gov/freedom250
media.author: NASA

media.text:
What's it like to fly NASA's experimental, quiet supersonic aircraft?

We're hosting a @Reddit AMA with our X-59 test pilots on Monday, May 18. Stop by and share your questions! teddit.net/r/nasa/
media.author: NASA

media.text:
"It’s great to be with you in the people’s house."

This week, NASA’s Artemis II crew visited the U.S. Capitol to speak with lawmakers, congressional staff, and special guests about their historic 10-day mission around the Moon and back.

And, yes, Rise was there too!
media.author: NASA

media.text:
To build on the Moon, we need construction materials that can withstand bitter cold – so @NASAglenn invented a machine that can recreate that environment for testing!

This high-powered refrigerator, or cryocooler, removes heat without using any liquid. go.nasa.gov/3PGqIR9
media.author: NASA

media.text: Teams stood down from today’s launch opportunity due to inclement weather. We're now targeting no earlier than 6:05 p.m. EDT Friday, May 15, for the launch. Details: go.nasa.gov/4uPQe5x
media.author: NASA

media.text: Planning is underway for our Artemis III mission, which will test the capabilities of NASA's Orion spacecraft and one or both commercial landers from @BlueOrigin and @SpaceX as they meet and dock in Earth's orbit. Details: go.nasa.gov/4nvfifB
media.author: NASA

media.text: LIVE: Experts talk about the next cargo launch to the @Space_Station. This delivery will include supplies and science experiments, including one that will evaluate how red blood cells and the spleen change in space to protect future astronauts. piped.video/live/-SejIZD6b8k
media.author: NASA

media.text:
Our next cargo mission to the @Space_Station is lifting off from Florida as soon as Tuesday, May 12.

Flying aboard: medical research to understand our blood and bones, a new space weather experiment, and supplies and hardware for the crew. Read more: go.nasa.gov/4wiuCjP
media.author: NASA

media.text:
Retiring at 100 years old? Not a chance! Here’s a sneak peek of Sir David Attenborough working on the narration for the upcoming BLUE PLANET III.
#DavidAttenborough100 #DavidAttenborough #BluePlanetIII
media.author: BBCEarth

media.text:
A-meow-zing 😻

@NASAWebb and @ChandraXray joined forces to deliver this view of the Cat's Paw Nebula. Different wavelengths combine to reveal young stars at the center of these dusty clouds.
media.author: NASA

media.text:
A lifetime dedicated to bringing the natural world closer to us all.
 
Happy birthday, Sir David Attenborough – from Planet Earth 💚🌍
 
#AttenboroughAt100 #DavidAttenborough100 #DavidAttenborough
media.author: BBCEarth

media.text:
From an unforgettable encounter with a blind baby rhino to a slow surprise with a sloth, these are 10 of Sir David Attenborough's most memorable wildlife experiences.
 
Read the full article here - bbc-earth.visitlink.me/ecnS4… 
 
#DavidAttenborough100 #DavidAttenborough
media.author: BBCEarth

media.text:
Look out for the @Space_Station the next time it passes over your town!

You might see it in the night sky the next time it's nearby — download our NASA app to get notified, or look it up on our website at go.nasa.gov/4wmkiav.
media.author: NASA

media.text: We're sending science experiments and supplies to the @Space_Station aboard an uncrewed SpaceX Dragon spacecraft as soon as Tuesday, May 12. Get the details and full schedule for our upcoming cargo launch: go.nasa.gov/3QP6B3z
media.author: NASA

media.text:
Finding real-life Tatooines ⭐⭐

Scientists studied data from our TESS telescope and found over two dozen planets that orbit pairs of stars! For Star Wars Day, learn more about how these planets outside our solar system are formed. go.nasa.gov/4d3wbJA
media.author: NASA

media.text:
Start your week with some new photos from Artemis II!

Though our journey around the Moon has ended, we're still retrieving plenty of new images. Keep an eye on our Artemis II multimedia gallery for image highlights from the mission: go.nasa.gov/4usiN8W
media.author: NASA

media.text:
Start your week with some new photos from Artemis II!

Though our journey around the Moon has ended, we're still retrieving plenty of new images. Keep an eye on our Artemis II multimedia gallery for image highlights from the mission: go.nasa.gov/4usiN8W
media.author: NASA

media.text:
Start your week with some new photos from Artemis II!

Though our journey around the Moon has ended, we're still retrieving plenty of new images. Keep an eye on our Artemis II multimedia gallery for image highlights from the mission: go.nasa.gov/4usiN8W
media.author: NASA

media.text:
Start your week with some new photos from Artemis II!

Though our journey around the Moon has ended, we're still retrieving plenty of new images. Keep an eye on our Artemis II multimedia gallery for image highlights from the mission: go.nasa.gov/4usiN8W
media.author: NASA

media.text:
What’s in the night sky for May?

In this month’s episode of “What’s Up,” see how to spot the Eta Aquarid meteor shower, find out when we’ll get a blue moon, and learn more about a celestial meetup between the Moon and Venus.

Happy skywatching! go.nasa.gov/4cP2lYU
media.author: NASA

media.text:
POV: you're rolling around on the Red Planet

You’re looking at six years on Mars in around two minutes. This timelapse contains images captured by our Curiosity rover between 2020 and 2026 from one of its navigation cameras. go.nasa.gov/4cH4mrt
media.author: NASA

media.text: Last is @Astro_ChrisW. Like Hathaway, he was part of the Flies astronaut class and is on his first trip to the orbiting lab. On his account, he posts his astrophotography.
media.author: NASA

media.text: Last is @Astro_ChrisW. Like Hathaway, he was part of the Flies astronaut class and is on his first trip to the orbiting lab. On his account, he posts his astrophotography.
media.author: NASA

media.text: Next is @Astro_Jessica. Prior to her current mission, she spent 205 days in space and completed three spacewalks. On her account, she posts photos of Earth views and everyday life in space.
media.author: NASA

media.text: Next is @Astro_Jessica. Prior to her current mission, she spent 205 days in space and completed three spacewalks. On her account, she posts photos of Earth views and everyday life in space.
media.author: NASA

media.text: First is @astro_hathaway, part of our 2021 astronaut class, known as the Flies. He is a seasoned pilot, but this trip marks his first visit to the station.
media.author: NASA

media.text:
Missing updates from the Artemis II crew? We have other humans in space you can follow!

Meet the NASA astronauts currently aboard the @Space_Station in the thread below 👇
media.author: NASA

media.text: Liftoff! Progress 95 took off from Baikonur at 6:21pm ET (2221 UTC). The uncrewed resupply spacecraft, carrying cargo for the @Space_Station, is set to dock with the orbiting laboratory on Monday, April 27.
media.author: NASA

media.text:
Let's see… a five-letter word for happiness: space! 🦀
 
@NASAHubble captures the intricate structure of the Krab, er, Crab Nebula in this image.
media.author: NASA

media.text:
This September, we're sending a new crew to the @Space_Station!

Meet the four members of our SpaceX Crew-13 mission, who will help us learn how to live in space while making life better back on Earth: go.nasa.gov/4cvxYYR
media.author: NASA

media.text:
Jordan is the 63rd country to sign the @NASAArtemis Accords! 🇯🇴

Countries that join our Artemis Accords commit to a set of principles for peaceful, transparent, and responsible space exploration. These partnerships will enable us to build the future of space together. go.nasa.gov/4u0qXVG
media.author: NASA

media.text:
Our Artemis II Reddit AMA is open for your questions! 

Our team will be back to answer them at 3:30pm ET (1930 UTC): teddit.net/comments/1stjt7m
media.author: NASA

media.text:
If you could meet the NASA team that worked on Artemis II, what would you like to ask?

Join our experts on Thursday, April 23, for a Reddit AMA ("Ask Me Anything") about the Moon mission: teddit.net/r/nasa/
media.author: NASA

media.text:
20 years ago, Planet Earth helped us see the world in a different way. It brought distant landscapes, hidden behaviours and rarely seen moments into our homes like never before 💚

Read how the patience, skill and persistence of the film crew made it all happen here - bbc-earth.visitlink.me/49FcE… 

#PlanetEarth #20thAnniversary #FilmingFirsts #FilmCrew
media.author: BBCEarth

media.text:
20 years ago, Planet Earth helped us see the world in a different way. It brought distant landscapes, hidden behaviours and rarely seen moments into our homes like never before 💚

Read how the patience, skill and persistence of the film crew made it all happen here - bbc-earth.visitlink.me/49FcE… 

#PlanetEarth #20thAnniversary #FilmingFirsts #FilmCrew
media.author: BBCEarth

media.text:
It's our home.

This Earth Day, see our planet as our Artemis II astronauts saw it with these new images from the mission.
media.author: NASA

media.text:
It's our home.

This Earth Day, see our planet as our Artemis II astronauts saw it with these new images from the mission.
media.author: NASA

media.text:
It's our home.

This Earth Day, see our planet as our Artemis II astronauts saw it with these new images from the mission.
media.author: NASA

media.text:
It's our home.

This Earth Day, see our planet as our Artemis II astronauts saw it with these new images from the mission.
media.author: NASA

media.text: Named after our first chief astronomer, Roman's deep, panoramic view of the cosmos will generate never-before-seen pictures. science.nasa.gov/mission/rom…
media.author: NASA

media.text:
Our Curiosity Mars rover has been studying the Red Planet since it first touched down on Gale Crater back in 2012.

Now, a new analysis has revealed the most diverse collection of organic molecules ever found on Mars—informing our search for ancient life: nasa.gov/missions/mars-scien…
media.author: NASA

media.text: The next delivery of NASA science and equipment to the @Space_Station is set for no earlier than May 12. In addition to experiments, these resupply missions also bring supplies that allow the crew aboard to continue living and working in low Earth orbit: go.nasa.gov/3Qe8wyp
media.author: NASA

media.text:
With Artemis II safely back on Earth, NASA has begun shipping the core stage for the Artemis III rocket to @NASAKennedy.

Artemis III will test rendezvous and docking capabilities needed to land Artemis IV astronauts on the Moon in 2028. go.nasa.gov/4e0uXkj
media.author: NASA

media.text: 5,000 light-years from Earth, stars are forming in the Trifid Nebula. Our orbiting @NASAHubble telescope is taking a close look: go.nasa.gov/4tX1BYL
media.author: NASA

media.text:
Welcome to the @NASAArtemis Accords, Latvia! 🇱🇻

Latvia is the 62nd country to sign the Artemis Accords—an international commitment aimed at enhancing the safety, transparency, and coordination of exploration on the Moon, Mars, and beyond: go.nasa.gov/4vHoZuU
media.author: NASA

media.text:
One week has passed since Artemis II returned to Earth after a historic mission around the Moon. We laughed, we cried, and we pushed the boundaries of what’s possible, setting the stage for future innovation and exploration.

We’re just getting started.
media.author: NASA

media.text: "Victor, Christina, Jeremy, just... thank you. This was an unbelievable adventure and it was made possible by this crew and the support of each other throughout the whole thing." - @Astro_Reid, commander of Artemis II.
media.author: NASA

media.text: “The difference now is, when we come before you now, we’ve done this together. We took your hearts with us, and your hearts lifted our hearts.” - @Astro_Christina on the positive impact of the mission.
media.author: NASA

A window to our world 🌎
This is a commercial channel from BBC Studios.
